violations.text: Cream Cheese was observed held at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Person in charge to keep cream cheese submerged into ice/ice bath for proper cold holding procedures. Per PIC, food was put out an hour prior to inspection. Food was relocated to refrigerator to rapidly cool. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/17/2025
No person in charge certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. FMC28298 is expired, PIC given application for CFSM. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 25 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Sanitizer solution was replaced and calibrated to proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Milk, cream cheese, and cheese was observed held at 65-70 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, foods were placed under 2 hours prior to inspection. Based on time and temperature food determined to be safe and did not need to be disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

Eggs are served undercooked/over easy at the breakfast buffet. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 07/01/2025

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024

The hand wash sink was observed being used as a dump and rinse sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024

Eggs were observed held at 105-113 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Inspector determined that eggs were out of proper temperature for less than two hours and did not need to be disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024

Yogurt and cut melon were observed held at 60 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Inspector determined that these items were out of proper temperature for less than two hours and did not need to be disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/02/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dish machine and three bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The establishment is lacking a serivce sink. Mop water and similar liquid wastes are contaminated with microorganisms and other filth. Waste water must be disposed of in a sanitary manner that will not contaminate food or food equipment. A service sink or curbed cleaning facility with a drain allows for such disposal. New Violation. Correct By: 07/16/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per person in charge, she has signed up for a food safety course. Proof of enrollment was given during time of inspection. Please email inspector proof upon completion of course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/03/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Please email inspector proof of enrollment in a food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023
Pretzel bites were observed adulterated (mold grown on them). Pretzel bites were voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Food was observed held at 43-60 degrees Fahrenheit in refrigerator #3. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been out of temperature since the day prior. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Refrigerator had an ambient air temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Refrigerator was taken out of service during time of inspection and must not be used until it is repaired/replaced and can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Per person in charge, a new refrigerator has already been ordered but has not been delivered yet. Please email inspector proof of repair/replacement of refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Food was observed held at 43-46 degrees Fahrenheit in refrigerator #2. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been out of temperature for more than 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of during time of inspection (see attached disposal form). Refrigerator had an ambient air temperature of 45 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Refrigerator was taken out of service during time of inspection and must not be used until it is repaired/replaced and can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Please email inspector proof or repair/replacement. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Milk was observed held at 67 degrees Fahrenheit in an ice bath on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, milk had been on the counter for about 2 hours. Milk was relocated to a refrigeration unit during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023
Establishment is lacking an allergy sign notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Per person in charge, she is in the process of taking the course. An invoice was provided during time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023
An employee was observed dumping food and washing equipment in the handwashing sink. Handwashing sinks must only be used for handwashing. New Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023
The handwashing sink in the kitchen is lacking a handwashing sink sign Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

Tomatoes were observed with mold on them. Food must be in good condition, safe, and unadulterated. Tomatoes were voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

Food was observed held at 43-53 degrees Fahrenheit in refrigerator #3. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the refrigerator since the day prior. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Refrigerator was taken out of service at time of inspection and must not be used until it is repaired or replaced. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

The door to the outside, located in the basement and front door, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/13/2023
There was not an allergy statement notifying customers of their obligation to notify staff if they have a food allergy. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment must enroll additional employees in a food safety course. Please email inspector proof of enrollment in a food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

There was no evidence that employees are required to report to the person in charge information about their health (illnesses, symptoms) as they relate to diseases that are transmissible through food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2023

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the bathroom. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
Ginger was observed with mold on it in the refrigerator. Ginger was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

Multiple food items (see temperature observations) were observed held at 47-52 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature was observed at 50 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Refrigerator was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Refrigeration temperature dial was set at the warmest setting during time of inspection. Per person in charge, establishment will call refrigeration services if the unit can not be adjusted to the proper temperature to store food at 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

Multiple food items (see temperature observations) were observed held at 43-47 degrees Fahrenheit in the refrigerator #3. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature was observed at 44 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Refrigerator was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Refrigeration temperature dial was set at the warmest setting during time of inspection. Per person in charge, establishment will call refrigeration services if the unit can not be adjusted to the proper temperature to store food at 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Per person in charge pest control services will be contacted. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

The door to the outside, located in the basement and the front door, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
Spices and potatoes were observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
Multiple food items were observed uncovered in the refrigeration units. Food must be covered during storage to prevent contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

Single-use articles (disposable cups) were observed stored on the floor in the dry storage area. Single-use articles must be stored at least 6 inches off the ground. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
